answer:
**San Hyan Kamahayanikan**: The cosmos (along with the body, its elements, aggregates, wisdoms, poisons, embryonic stages, and all phenomena) is centrally organized as a maṇḍala around the five Tathāgatas (Pañcatathāgata)—Vairocana, Akṣobhya, Ratnasambhava, Amitābha, and Amoghasiddhi—who emanate all reality, from the Ratnatraya (Three Jewels) to the five elements, five skandhas, five wisdoms, and creation itself.[^1][^2][^3]

**Viṣṇu Smṛti**: Vishnu (as the cosmic Boar/Varāha) creates and orders the entire universe—seven netherworlds and upper worlds, continents, oceans, guardians of places/worlds/directions, sages, Vedas, rivers, mountains, trees, gods, anti-gods, various beings (Piśācas to humans), and sacrifices—then sustains the Earth through the dharma-practicing humans of the four varṇas and four āśramas.[^4][^5]

**Harder to substitute**: San Hyan Kamahayanikan. Its structure is a rigid tantric maṇḍala defined by exactly *five* Tathāgatas (with fixed seed-syllables, consorts, mappings to quintuple categories like the five elements/skandhas/wisdoms/poisons, and emanations); substituting even one (e.g., adding/removing a Tathāgata) fractures the doctrinal symmetry and initiatory system.[^6] Viṣṇu Smṛti's cosmology is a narrative enumeration of roles/entities under Vishnu's creative act, sustained by varṇa/āśrama dharma; one term/office (e.g., a guardian) is readily substitutable without collapsing the hierarchy or purpose.[^7]

[^1]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-638b90](https://falsafa.thothica.com/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-638b90) (Lords of the Five Tathāgatas).

[^2]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-f72091](https://falsafa.thothica.com/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-f72091) (Pañcatathāgata as body of reality, emanating all).

[^3]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-204881](https://falsafa.thothica.com/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-204881) (seed-syllables creating the Five Tathāgatas).

[^4]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 1/translation/](https://falsafa.thothica.com/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/) (Vishnu's creation as Boar).

[^5]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-199ee0](https://falsafa.thothica.com/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-199ee0) (Earth supported by varṇas/āśramas).

[^6]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-ac0261](https://falsafa.thothica.com/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-ac0261) (fivefold mappings, e.g., elements).

[^7]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-1af09a](https://falsafa.thothica.com/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-1af09a) (enumerated cosmic roles).
